summary: Heavily obfuscated bulk email/SMS toolkit with license phone-home; abuse-tooling concern
details: Package presents as an 'advanced email sender' with red-team keyword and ships ~40 heavily obfuscated JavaScript files under.ad/ (javascript-obfuscator string-array + RC4 wrapping). Feature set spans bulk SMS via SMTP-to-SMS gateways, Microsoft 365 / Google OAuth device-code authentication, IMAP contact extraction, DKIM key generation, and redirect/Wayback scanning — capability profile consistent with phishing and bulk-spam tooling directed at third parties. Postinstall writes only a Windows.cmd shim (`%prefix%/nolimit.cmd` invoking `node <entryFile> %*`) on win32 — no remote fetch, no execution of attacker-controlled bytes, no environment scraping. The CLI's `activate` and per-command `serverVerify` paths POST `{key, hwid, hostname, platform}` to `http://api.nolimitent.xyz:4100/verify` and `/activate`; the destination is the package's own publisher domain and the call fires only on user-invoked CLI commands, not at install or import. Concerns for review: (a) the toolkit's documented purpose is sending bulk email/SMS to third parties, which is abuse-adjacent rather than a developer-facing library; (b) all functional code is obfuscated, hiding behavior from reviewers; (c) license check sends a host identifier over cleartext HTTP. Installer-side attack signals (install-time RCE, credential theft from `~/.aws`/`~/.ssh`/`~/.npmrc`, env-var scraping, dropper, silent-relay, backdoor) are not present.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
